audit: JPEG spec compliance (ITU-T T.81 / T.871 JFIF / T.872 Exif) for pure C# encoder+decoder (JpegSharp)
status: AUDIT COMPLETE — all 17 sections reviewed (compliance + adversarial + test); results documented

sections_reviewed.section: DHT Huffman-table segment parsing & table build
Component handling, sampling factors, MCU structure & block order
Huffman entropy decode: DC diff, AC run/size, sign-extend, EOB, ZRL
Progressive: spectral selection + successive approximation + refinement
Encoder Huffman generation (standard+optimized) + bitstream + byte stuffing
Encoder marker emission + overall file-structure compliance
Metadata: JFIF (APP0), Exif (APP1), ICC (APP2 multi-seg), Adobe (APP14), COM

known_issues.issue: Decoder must reject Pq (precision nibble) not in {0,1}.
known_issues.specRef: T.81 B.2.4.1 (Pq = 0 -> 8-bit, Pq = 1 -> 16-bit; reject Pq not in {0,1})

known_issues.issue: Tc (table class) must be in {0,1}; values 2..15 are reserved and must be rejected (T.81 B.2.4.2, Table B.5)
Sum of the 16 BITS counts (total number of codes / HUFFVAL bytes) must be <= 256
known_issues.specRef: T.81 B.2.4.2 (sum of Li <= 256) / Annex F.2.2

known_issues.issue: Sum of Hi*Vi <= 10 for interleaved scans (T.81 A.2.2 / B.2.2 max 10 data units per MCU)
Reject unsupported precisions; valid DCT precision is 8 (baseline) or 8/12 (extended/progressive) only
known_issues.specRef: ITU-T T.81 B.2.2 (P: baseline 8; extended/progressive 8 or 12)

known_issues.issue: Must reject DAC (Define Arithmetic Conditioning marker)
known_issues.specRef: ITU-T T.81 B.2.4.3 (DAC); requirement to reject DAC

known_issues.issue: Reject Ns=0 or Ns>4 (T.81 B.2.3: 1 <= Ns <= 4)
Decoder must handle missing/misordered RST gracefully.
known_issues.specRef: ITU-T T.81 F.1.2.3 / E.1.1 (restart markers, graceful resync)

known_issues.issue: Non-interleaved (single-component) scans must use per-component dims: blocksPerLine=ceil(xi/8), xi=ceil(X*Hi/Hmax); blocksPerCol=ceil(yi/8), yi=ceil(Y*Vi/Vmax); MCU = 1 data unit (T.81 A.2.2, A.2.4).
A baseline sequential frame may be coded as multiple (typically non-interleaved) scans; a decoder must process every scan (T.81 A.2, B.2.3).

StoreBlock (BaselineDecoder.cs:464 8-bit, :478 high-precision) computes `value = (int)Math.Round(spatial) + center` in unchecked int arithmetic (net10.0, no CheckForOverflowUnderflow). Dequantized coefficients reach ~2.147e9 each (short coeff up to 32767 times ushort quant step up to 65535, Quantizer.cs:45), so an IDCT output pixel can exceed int.MaxValue. The double->int conversion saturates to int.MaxValue, then `+ center` overflows and wraps to a large NEGATIVE int, so Math.Clamp(negative,0,max) returns 0 instead of the required 2^P-1. The explicit clamp-to-max requirement is inverted for large-positive IDCT results: a should-be-saturated-white pixel decodes as black (0). The reviewer's 'Correct. clamp performed after level shift' finding overlooked that the level-shift addition itself overflows before the clamp sees a sane value.
T.871 YCbCr<->RGB centered at 2^(P-1); high-precision conversion must be correct across supported precisions (9-16 bit)
known_issues.specRef: T.871 YCbCr/RGB conversion; internal support of P=9..16

known_issues.issue: T.81 B.2.3 / G.1: a progressive DC scan may be non-interleaved (single component), in which case data units are traversed in that component's own block raster (ceil(actualW/8) x ceil(actualH/8)), not the full-image MCU grid.
Quality scaling must clamp elements to [1,255] for 8-bit OR [1,65535] for 16-bit depending on output precision.
known_issues.specRef: T.81 Annex K.1 scaling; B.2.4.1 (Pq/Qk element precision)

known_issues.issue: COM arbitrary bytes must round-trip losslessly
known_issues.specRef: T.81 B.2.4.5 (COM = arbitrary bytes); task requirement 'COM arbitrary bytes'

known_issues.issue: ICC APP2 chunks must reassemble correctly; reader must tolerate duplicate/malformed chunk sets without producing corrupt output
known_issues.specRef: ICC.1 Annex B / T.872 (ICC_PROFILE\0 + seqno + count, reassemble in order); task requirement 'reassemble in order' and 'tolerate duplicate'
ITU-T T.81 E.1.4 / F.1.4 (restart marker sequence RSTm, m = 0..7 mod 8)

known_issues.issue: no unbounded allocation / fail safely with a typed exception
known_issues.specRef: T.81 B.2.2 (dimensions up to 65535x65535); robustness: typed failure + bounded allocation
